key_insight: A D-gun deposited Al2O3–TiO2 coating substantially improves the tribological performance of Ti-6Al-4V by reducing wear and promoting a protective tribo-layer.
engineering_relevance: The study shows that detonation-sprayed ceramic coatings can extend the wear resistance of titanium alloys used in aerospace, biomedical, and marine components.
method: Applied ~200 µm Al2O3–TiO2 coating on Ti-6Al-4V using the detonation gun process.
Evaluated wear with ASTM G99 pin-on-disc tests against EN31 steel at 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min.
Built ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from sliding parameters.
evidence_signal: Scanning electron microscopy showed reduced wear on coated samples, and random forest gave the lowest mean percentage error and mean squared error among the tested models.
watch_item: Performance depends on controlled D-gun processing and the tested load/speed range, so broader validation is needed before generalizing to other operating conditions.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
